hard.next_step: 6/2/26 MQ Sending out 3rd request for discovery meeting. Ferid has not responded to multiple attempts to schedule deep discovery and demo.
hard.ais_why: Ferid explicitly said he was 'curious to see agentic AI end-to-end automation generating savings without human intervention' and asked whether AI negotiation extends beyond catalog items to services. SF description confirms interest in Merlin and AI use cases for ANA and iRisk in SRPM.

north_star_verdict.headline: 88 days since the only buyer conversation (09 Apr intro) and three rep outreach attempts (22 Apr, 15 May, 02 Jun) ignored. No demo, no second stakeholder, no two-way engagement. Deal is cold at early stage.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 09 Apr 2026, Qualified since 10 Apr 2026 (~87 days in Qualified). 209 days to a 31 Jan 2027 close. Last verified BUYER engagement was the intro call on 09 Apr 2026 (~88 days ago). Rep-side outreach on 22 Apr, 15 May, and 02 Jun 2026 drew zero buyer reply. No deliverable executed and no buyer-facing milestone met in ~88 days. Cold for 60+ days forces Off Track regardless of the long close runway.
evidence: Last buyer engagement: intro call 09 Apr 2026 (~88 days ago); Ferid Znaidia expressed AI curiosity and agreed to review the deck and share it with upper management, target demo late May / early June (Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026, SF Next_Step__c 09 Apr).
Rep outreach 22 Apr 2026: scheduled deep-dive discovery for week of 18 May, demo end of May (SF Next_Step__c 22 Apr).
Rep outreach 15 May 2026: scheduling deep-dive discovery, trying to pull in additional stakeholders (SF Next_Step__c 15 May, Clari email task 15 May).
Rep outreach 02 Jun 2026: third request for discovery meeting, Ferid not responding to multiple attempts (SF Next_Step__c 02 Jun, Clari email task 02 Jun).
Zero buyer reply to any of three outreach attempts; no demo scheduled, no stakeholder expansion, no deliverable executed since the 09 Apr intro call.
Single-threaded to Ferid Znaidia (Sr Category Manager, Indirect Procurement); no OpportunityContactRole records on the opp and no upper-management contact ever surfaced.
The 209-day close runway is irrelevant while the buyer has been silent for ~88 days; the deal is cold, not merely slowing.

metrics.narrative: Ferid Znaidia named a clear qualitative driver on the 09 Apr 2026 intro call: 'my area is really focused on cost improvement and cost savings' and he framed eProcurement, AI leverage and data analysis as 'different areas that could lead to process improvement or cost cutting'. No number was ever attached: no baseline indirect spend, no target savings percentage, no headcount or cycle-time figure. The one quantified data point came from Zycus, not the buyer (Marc cited 'a savings of about 3 to 5%' from the Anna negotiation agent). The business case is therefore real but unquantified, and quantifying it against Ferid's HR and professional-services categories is a required move once contact is re-established.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. Ferid Znaidia is a Sr Category Manager who repeatedly deferred the decision upward: 'I'll share it with my upper management and see if there is any interest in pursuing' and 'really, all comes down to the cost' (Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026). He owns HR and professional-services categories only, not the budget, and named no individual above him. There are no OpportunityContactRole records and no MEDDPICC economic-buyer field value, so the budget owner is entirely unknown. The evidence shows only that an unnamed 'upper management' group controls whether the evaluation proceeds; naming and reaching that person is the single highest-value gap to close.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's stated criteria are cost/price first and AI-driven automation second. Ferid asked 'what's the cost for a subscription?' three separate times and closed with 'really, all comes down to the cost' (Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026). His technical interest was specific: whether the agentic AI 'goes A to Z without human intervention and comes up with a savings', and crucially whether autonomous negotiation extends 'beyond catalog items to services' like temporary labor, which is his category. Implementation time also mattered ('usually stuff like this takes up to 6 months'). These are early, inferred-from-one-call criteria, not a documented requirements list, and no formal RFP/RFI or scorecard has been shared.

decision_process.narrative: The only visible process step is Ferid's own two-stage plan from the 09 Apr 2026 call: review the deck himself, then 'share it with my upper management and see if there is any interest in pursuing', then schedule a solution-consultant call and a demo. That process stalled at step one: three rep attempts to book the deep-dive got no reply, so we do not know whether the deck ever reached upper management or what they said. No approval chain, no evaluation timeline, no committee and no buying stage beyond Ferid have been confirmed. The process is effectively unknown until a second, more senior stakeholder engages.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics exist yet, which is normal for a Qualified early-stage deal with no demo delivered. Nothing is known about MSA/SOW paper ownership, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements or any current-contract expiration that could act as a forcing function. Ferid did flag implementation timing as a concern ('usually stuff like this takes up to 6 months'), but that is a delivery-duration question, not a paper step. Visibility here will only come after a demo and a commercial conversation with the budget owner.

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed, category-scoped pain. Post-divestiture, Commscope operates as a standalone company ('we're still a standalone company, we have to have our own tools') and must run its own procurement stack rather than inherit the acquirer Amphenol's. Ferid owns HR and professional-services sourcing (temporary labor, HR benefits, training, talent acquisition) where the current SAP Ariba catalog-first model is a poor fit for non-catalog services, and current supplier scorecards are manual ('a template that you do things manually'). He is accountable for cost improvement and savings in those categories. The pain is real and owned by Ferid, but its urgency versus other post-divestiture priorities is unproven given his silence since 09 Apr 2026.

champion.narrative: No champion. Ferid Znaidia was warm and curious on the 09 Apr 2026 intro call and committed to reviewing the deck and sharing it upward, but he has produced no advocacy since: no introductions, no internal socialization we can confirm, and no reply to three outreach attempts across 22 Apr, 15 May and 02 Jun 2026. A champion actively advocates, provides access to power and drives the internal agenda; Ferid has done none of these in ~88 days and never named anyone above him. The deal is single-threaded to a contact who has gone quiet, which is the primary reason it is Off Track. Building or replacing the champion ranks ahead of any commercial step.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the confirmed incumbent. Ferid confirmed Commscope runs SAP S/4HANA and Ariba ('do you use Ariba as well? Yes') on the 09 Apr 2026 call, and he positioned Zycus as a peer rather than a challenger: 'seems like you guys are also side by side with Ariba and SAP for similar functions, similar outcome'. He declined to say whether he was happy with Ariba or looking to replace it ('I won't be able to share that'), which signals no active dissatisfaction we can exploit. The realistic competitive threat is not a rival bake-off but status-quo inertia plus Ariba retention by default: with no demo delivered and the buyer silent, the entrenched SAP stack stays in place unchallenged. Our wedge is Merlin agentic AI for non-catalog HR/professional-services sourcing where Ariba's catalog-first model underperforms, activated by the post-divestiture standalone-tools mandate.

deal_movement.summary: The single buyer touch was the 09 Apr 2026 intro call; every event since is rep-side. Ferid never replied to the 22 Apr, 15 May or 02 Jun outreach, so no forward progress has occurred and the deal has sat in Qualified for ~87 days.

items.change: Intro call held; Ferid curious on Merlin agentic AI and services-scope negotiation, agreed to review the deck and share with upper management
Rep sent deck; scheduled deep-dive discovery for week of 18 May with demo to follow end of May (no buyer reply)
Rep second attempt to schedule deep-dive discovery and pull in additional stakeholders (no buyer reply)
Rep third request for a discovery meeting; logged that Ferid has not responded to multiple attempts

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and, with the buyer silent since 09 Apr 2026, the single strongest current threat is status-quo retention of that SAP stack by default. Ferid confirmed Ariba on S/4HANA on 09 Apr 2026 but disclosed no dissatisfaction ('I won't be able to share that') and framed Zycus as 'side by side with Ariba', so we have earned no lead. No other vendor (Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer, Ivalua, Zip) was named in any source. The win path is displacing Ariba on non-catalog HR/professional-services fit via Merlin agentic AI, but zero progress has been made against the incumbent to date.

competitors.how_we_win: Anchor Merlin agentic AI and the Anna autonomous negotiation agent to Ferid's non-catalog HR and professional-services sourcing, where Ariba's catalog-first model underperforms; use the post-divestiture standalone-tools mandate as the forcing event and quantify savings on his manual scorecard and RFX processes.

competitors.name: Status quo / do nothing
competitors.quote: Rep note: 'Ferid has not responded to multiple attempts to schedule deep discovery and demo' after three requests across 88 days.
competitors.how_we_win: Do-nothing here means Commscope keeps running indirect sourcing on the existing SAP/Ariba stack and never prioritizes the evaluation; break it with a single high-impact HR-services savings use case and a second, more senior procurement stakeholder to validate urgency.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The buyer's stated expectations (cost/subscription transparency, AI automation that extends to services, reasonable implementation time) are broadly aligned with Zycus strengths, but every one is untested because no demo or pricing has been delivered.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, single-threaded, cold deal against an entrenched SAP/Ariba incumbent. The win levers are re-establishing contact, reaching power above Ferid, and landing a services-specific AI proof point.
flags: Break the single thread: Ferid is not the budget owner and has gone silent, so identify and email one procurement or finance leader above him to revive and validate the evaluation.
Lead re-engagement with a services-sourcing AI proof point (Anna on non-catalog HR/professional services), the exact gap Ferid probed versus Ariba's catalog-first model.
Attach a quantified savings hypothesis to Ferid's HR/professional-services spend to convert generic cost interest into a business case that upper management will fund.

AIS is Moderate (7). The positioning is adequate but under-proven: no demo has shown the agentic flow, and the key services-vs-catalog question Ferid raised is still unanswered.

The calls corroborate a genuine but early customer-driven AI pull: Ferid was 'curious to see agentic AI end-to-end automation generating savings without human intervention' and probed whether negotiation extends to services. The interest is real but shallow (one intro call, no demo), so the Moderate rating is fair, not under-rated.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A cold, single-threaded early-stage deal: one warm intro call on 09 Apr 2026, then ~88 days of silence from Ferid Znaidia (who is not the budget owner) across three ignored outreach attempts, with SAP Ariba entrenched. The one thing between us and any progress is getting a live buyer back on the phone and reaching power above Ferid.

win_position.text: The deal sits low because the only buyer contact, Ferid Znaidia, is not the budget owner and has gone silent for ~88 days, leaving SAP Ariba entrenched and unchallenged with no demo delivered.
No economic buyer is named: Ferid deferred every decision to an unnamed 'upper management' on 09 Apr 2026 and we have no path to them.
Ferid framed Zycus as 'side by side with Ariba ... similar functions, similar outcome' (09 Apr 2026), so we have not established differentiated value against the incumbent.

The one positive is a genuine, customer-driven AI pull: Ferid asked to see agentic automation go 'A to Z without human intervention' and whether it covers services, which is our wedge if we re-engage.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is negative: three rep attempts (22 Apr, 15 May, 02 Jun 2026) to book the deep-dive drew no reply and no demo was ever scheduled.
The account has held in Qualified for ~87 days with no seniority rise, no customer-named dates, and no buyer-requested next meeting.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer's only commitment, to review the deck and share it upward (09 Apr 2026), was never confirmed acted upon, and he has since ignored three follow-ups.
Commitment is effectively zero: no OpportunityContactRole records, no second stakeholder, and the decision explicitly parked with an unreachable 'upper management'.

deal_risk.text: Ghost risk is the dominant threat: a single cold contact who does not own budget means the deal can quietly die with no competitive loss, just do-nothing on the existing SAP/Ariba stack.
No quantified business case exists to force prioritization; Ferid reduced everything to cost ('all comes down to the cost') with no figures on the table.
